output.title: Security Deposit Recovery Sequence: From Demand Letter to Full Refund
output.meta_description: Landlord withholding your deposit? Know your tenant rights, dispute illegal deductions, and send a demand letter that forces your landlord to pay up fast.
content_outline: Section 1: The Reality Check — Why Your Landlord Is Withholding Your Security Deposit (And Why They Might Be Wrong)
Section 2: Know Your Rights — What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
Section 3: The Dispute Process — What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
Section 4: The Demand Letter — How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Section 5: Escalation Tactics — Next Steps If Your Deposit Is Still Not Returned
key_points: Most state laws require landlords to return security deposits within 14–60 days and provide an itemized deduction list
Normal wear and tear, unpaid rent, and cleaning are the most common areas of dispute—tenants often win when they challenge invalid deductions
A professionally written demand letter citing state-specific landlord-tenant law is the single most effective pre-litigation recovery tool
DepositBack automates state-compliant security deposit demand letters to remove guesswork and increase refund speed
output.cta: Stop letting your landlord keep your money. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter in under 5 minutes and start your recovery today.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return security deposits, and who are actively searching online for legal rights, dispute letters, and fast recovery solutions.
